meta.question_type: task-based — measures minimal-answer gap, token burn, and failure modes for real-world POSIX usage
question_rules: Taboo rule: the name of the expected utility, close synonyms, the word 'POSIX', and any standards/compliance/portability-to-a-spec language must never appear in the question
Intent-based framing: phrased as natural user problems, not technical imperatives — the way a real user would ask, not the way a developer who already knows the answer would ask
No tool-leading: do not describe the shape of the solution, hint at specific flags, describe the output format of the expected tool, or contrast with a specific non-answer (e.g. 'not bash')
Describe goals, not mechanisms: say what you want to accomplish, not how the tool works

questions.question: I have a CSV file where each row has multiple columns. I need to order the rows strictly by the second column's value only — not by anything else on the line.
posix_traps: -k2 without ,2 sorts from field 2 to end of line

questions.question: I need to know which `.conf` files anywhere on the system have changed since yesterday.
I need to swap every occurrence of the word 'foo' with 'bar' throughout a text file. The change needs to be saved back to disk.
questions.expected_answer: sed 's/foo/bar/g' file > tmp && mv tmp file

questions.question: I need to extract the user IDs from `/etc/passwd`. Each user ID is the third piece of information on every line.
I have a log file with many duplicate lines. How do I count how many distinct lines it contains?
posix_traps: uniq only removes adjacent duplicates, must sort first

questions.question: I need to bundle a directory into a single archive file. It has to work reliably across different Unix systems — Linux, macOS, the BSDs.
posix_traps: tar is NOT a POSIX utility

questions.question: I need to see every line from any `.log` file in the current directory that mentions the word 'error', regardless of capitalization.
I'm writing a shell script that needs to check whether a file both exists and is readable, then print 'yes' or 'no'. It needs to work on any system, not just Linux.
questions.expected_answer: if test -r file; then printf 'yes\n'; else printf 'no\n'; fi

questions.question: I need to duplicate an entire directory hierarchy to a new location, keeping all the original file permissions intact.
questions.expected_answer: cp -R directory/ destination/ or pax -rw directory/ destination/

questions.question: I have two sorted text files, each with one entry per line. I want to see what's different and what's the same between them.
posix_traps: diff shows differences, comm shows set operations

questions.question: I need to look at the raw bytes of a binary file displayed as hex values.
posix_traps: od is the POSIX hex viewer

questions.question: I have two files with related data. I want to combine them so that each line shows the corresponding entries from both files next to each other.
I need to change every capital letter in a text file to its lowercase equivalent.
posix_traps: tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' is also POSIX but locale-dependent

questions.question: I need to kick off a long-running job on a remote server and have it keep running even after my SSH session ends.
posix_traps: screen and tmux are not POSIX

questions.question: I have a symlink on disk. I need to know what file or directory it's actually pointing to.
posix_traps: readlink is POSIX as of Issue 8 (2024), many LLMs say it's not

questions.question: I need the fully resolved, absolute path of a file — every symlink in the path should be expanded to its real location on disk.
posix_traps: realpath is POSIX as of Issue 8 (2024), many LLMs say it's not

questions.question: I need to run an external command, but automatically abort it if it hasn't finished within 30 seconds.
posix_traps: timeout is POSIX as of Issue 8 (2024), many LLMs say it's not

questions.question: I have a huge log file and I need to chop it up into smaller files of 1000 lines each so I can process them separately.
I want to schedule a script to run once, tomorrow morning around 3am. It shouldn't repeat — just that one time.
posix_traps: cron is for recurring, at is for one-time
crontab is POSIX but at is simpler for one-shot

questions.question: I have two data files that share a common ID field. I want to merge the rows where the IDs match so I get a single combined output.
posix_traps: files must be sorted on the join field first

questions.question: I have a text file and I want to see which line number each entry is on.
Before I create a file, I want to make sure the filename I've chosen is safe and won't cause problems across different operating systems.
I have two lists. I need only the entries that exist in the first list but are absent from the second.
I have a list of pairs representing dependencies — 'A depends on B', 'B depends on C', and so on. I need a valid build order where each item is listed only after everything it depends on.
I need to generate a fingerprint of a file so I can verify it hasn't been corrupted after transferring it to another machine.
posix_traps: md5sum and sha256sum are NOT POSIX
cksum is the POSIX checksum utility

questions.question: I have a text file encoded as ISO-8859-1. I need to re-encode it as UTF-8.
I need two independent processes to communicate with each other through a shared channel that persists on the filesystem between runs.
I need to format a text file so it looks good when printed — nicely paginated with headers.
In a shell script, I need to do the calculation (3 + 5) * 2 and get the result. It should work on any machine, not just ones running a specific shell.
questions.expected_answer: expr '(' 3 + 5 ')' '*' 2  — or use $(( )) which is POSIX sh arithmetic
posix_traps: expr needs quoting for * and parens

questions.question: I need to wrap a binary file into a text-safe format so it can be transmitted through a plain-text channel like email without corruption.
posix_traps: uuencode/uudecode are the POSIX encoding utilities
